item.title: 与 Phoenix (Elixir) 实时同步 | Realtime Sync with Phoenix (Elixir)
item.description:
华丽的

发表于我们最喜欢的产品开发者 Theo 为他的应用Ping.gg使用 Pusher 和其他一些 websocket 工具

我们最喜欢的枫叶正在使用 Liveblocks 和 Convex，等等

调查实时同步应用程序状态并偶然发现了这条推文。

我喜欢@liveviewjs如何将组件与它们的服务器操作结合在一个文件中，这样您就不需要任何事件的 API。

所以我研究了 LiveView，它是建立在 Elixir lang 的 Phoenix Web 框架上的。 这让我想起了这次采访 - Ockam 与 Mrinal Wadhwa与 Ockham 老板交谈，特别是在 @26:15 他们为什么使用 Elixir 以及与替代解决方案相比的担忧。 这使我得出结论，Phoenix 基于 Elixir 构建的框架是用于消息传递或实时同步应用程序状态的框架。

视频直播 - 一个独立但不完全正交（不相关）的主题

该视频是一系列精彩的 Tech Bytes 的一部分，讨论了视频直播的类似主题。
无论您是从摄像机捕获视频还是从对象存储中提供视频，通过网络以块形式进行流式传输的过程都是相同的。 它类似于从 fs 提供 MD(X) 与来自远程 API 或 CMS 的数据。 提取步骤向前的逻辑保持不变。

除了通过 USDC 将英镑兑换成美元之外，web3 还有一个合法的用例：视频爱流。

克里斯蒂安·莱克纳—— 一旦暂停，bronifty 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，bronifty 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

举报其他不当行为

原文:

bronifty

Posted on Oct 22

Our fav product dev Theo uses Pusher and some other websocket tools for his app Ping.gg

Ping is so good that you can't actually tell @linusgsebastian is calling in remotely

Our fav maple leaf is using Liveblocks and Convex, among other things

pubsub is not a solution, it's an implementation detail

Investigating realtime sync app state and stumbled upon this tweet.

I love how @liveviewjs couples components with their server actions in one file, allowing you to not need an API for any event.

So I looked into LiveView and it is built on Elixir lang's Phoenix web framework. That reminded me of this interview - Ockam with Mrinal Wadhwa with the Ockham boss who talks, specifically at @26:15 why they are using Elixir and the concerns around it compared to alternate solutions. This leads me to the conclusion that Phoenix built on Elixir is the framework to use for messaging or live syncing app state.

Video Live Streaming - a separate but not completely orthogonal (unrelated) subject

This video, which is part of a great series of Tech Bytes, discusses a similar subject of video live streaming.
TLDR; Whether you are capturing video from a camera or serving it from object storage, the process of streaming in chunks over the wire will be the same. It's similar to serving MD(X) from fs vs data from a remote API or CMS. Logic from the extraction step forward remains unchanged.

Web3 Version - LivePeer

There is 1 legit use case for web3 besides trading GBP for USD via USDC: video love streaming.

Live peer (or should I say love peer) distributes the workload from a centralized server to many nodes which -at once- 1. serve data & 2. get paid in tokens


LivePeer

.

Christian Lechner - Oct 22

item.title: 强大的开发人员所做的让他们的团队疯狂的事情 | Things Strong Developers Do That Drive Their Team Crazy
item.description:
珍陈

发表于经过多年的从头到脚以及无数个夜晚的拉扯、调试……您已经解锁了您在开始编程时无法想象的超学习水平！

任何一个问题怎么可能如此可行？

为什么人们不像你一样优秀、富有成效、积极主动和积极进取？

他们为什么不互相交流，为什么不希望团队得到最好的？

我注意到，在追逐目标或里程碑方面形成狭隘的视野真的很容易，并且忘记了你的团队的感受。

如果他们没有保持一致并且关系不顺利，他们实际上会给你他们最糟糕的！

事后看来，我相信我自己的急躁和缺乏同理心让我成为了一名糟糕的本科老师。 我希望每个人都能分享我对我的学科的热情。

有些人上学就像他们必须洗衣服一样。 有些人工作只是为了做好工作，这绝对没问题！

我一直在思考Patrick Kuo关于开发人员在过渡到领导团队时面临的挑战和错误的演讲。

当我认为自己是团队中最严谨的人时，我做出了许多有缺陷的假设……我能够通过不间断的入职和一对一的时间来影响他人做得更好——就像我一样！

在我与不同思想流派的领导/资深人士共事的时候，我发现郭的演讲引起了我的共鸣，以下是一些令人信服的事情：

  • 只是告诉人们该做什么而不考虑他们的兴趣或优势，而且没有花时间去了解他们的观点和工作风格是由什么塑造的——很可能是他们自己的创伤和他人可能有缺陷的领导风格的经历。

  • 做出所有技术决策，从而限制团队感觉他们可以影响结果。

    • 在某些情况下，需要快速做出决定，尤其是在非常紧迫的时间表上。 但是，听取他人支持或拒绝某项决定的理由应该是您了解您所做决定的影响的持续责任的一部分。

  • 重构其他人刚刚完成的功能以满足个人对标准的期望，这使刚刚完成工单的开发人员士气低落

  • 在不与团队就期望进行沟通的情况下按量做更多的工作，这会阻止人们在不同难度的任务上通过尝试和失败来学习——并且还会建立一个循环，让他们期望你会弥补他们的懈怠。

    • 我已经收集到我的工作是向其他开发人员展示如何做得更好......并让他们决定是否要效仿。 如果他们经历了失败，这是他们需要意识到并拥有作为他们职业生涯的一部分的东西。

  • 无需入职即可简单地委派任务。 单个团队成员需要上下文来完成他们的任务。 如果您无法找到其他人与他们同步，那么您有责任为他们配备完成任务所需的资源。

  • 只做自己感兴趣的工作，而不是思考如何支持队友和发展每个人的技术和个人优势。

    • 这意味着允许其他人解决具有挑战性的技术问题，而不是因为你对它们感兴趣或对它们有很高的投资而去做。 你还有其他跨团队、跨部门的事情要做。 当他们无法弄清楚时，您可以介入帮助。

以下是我认为对我自己和团队的一般生产力特别低效的一些：

  • 编写详细的票据和文档，并期望人们只是阅读并效仿，而不是帮助他们理解为什么写作主动性对团队很重要，并发展写下指令的肌肉，以便在他人必须审查或审查时让他们的生活更轻松测试他们的工作。

  • 期望人们立即回复或做出决定，或以与您相同的速度和风格工作。 你是可交付成果的“失败者/人”，但你的工作也是预测其他人的舒适程度和工作节奏，以及他们如何感受到动力和授权。

我在这篇文章中提到了我正在努力改进的弱点。 每次都有机会反馈一次，不适用于不同的情况，也可能过度纠正。

我相信，反思失误为人们创造了机会，让人们为你自己对自己的表现的评估是否准确或过于挑剔提供另一种观点。

我喜欢与人合作，这是一个相当大的转变，我从一个性格内向、斗志昂扬的独奏艺术家转变为感觉我需要一群人一起工作来创作任何东西。

所有这些都是奢侈品和特权，能够冥想我如何做我喜欢做的事，更好地工作。

对于有或没有权威的开发人员、质量分析师和 PM 以及技术领导者的问题：

  • 你注意到自己做了什么来应对压力，这对你的团队有何影响？

  • 你曾尝试做些什么来让他人感到安全并了解如何更好地合作？

  • 你是如何处理沟通较少或性格内向的人，他们的沟通方式不同？

  • 什么时候你意识到一个团队没有像你想象的那样运作，你做了什么或改变了你的方法来帮助他们？

塞巴斯蒂安 Tiedtke - 一旦暂停，jenc 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，jenc 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，jenc 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

原文:

jen chan

Posted on Oct 22

After years of nose-to-keyboard and countless nights of hairpulling, debugging... you've unlocked a level of ultra-learning you couldn't have imagined when you began programming!

How can any one problem be so doable?

Why aren't people as excellent, productive, proactive, and motivated as you?

Why don't they communicate with each other, why don't they want the best for the team?

I've noticed it's really easy to develop tunnel vision about chasing a goal or milestone, and to forget about how your team feels.

If they're not aligned and relationships are not going well, they will in fact, give you their even-worst!

In hindsight, I believe my own impatience and lack of empathy made me a terrible undergraduate teacher. I expected everyone to share the passion I had for my discipline.

Some people go to school like they have to do laundry. Some people work just to do an okay-job, and that is absolutely ok!

I have been reflecting on this Patrick Kuo talk on challenges and mistakes that developers face as they transition into leading a team.

I have made many flawed assumptions when I thought I was the most rigorous on a team… that I would be able to, through non-stop onboarding and one-on-one time, influence others to do better -- to be like me!

Here are a few things I've found compelling about Kuo's talk that resonated with me in the times I've worked with leads/senior of differing schools-of-thought:

  • Just telling people what to do without considering their interests or strengths, and furthermore not taking the time to understand what their perspective and work style is shaped by-- very likely their own traumas and experiences of others' possibly-flawed leadership styles.

  • Making all the tech decisions, therefore limiting the team from feeling like they can influence outcomes.

    • There are scenarios where quick decisions need to be made, especially on a very tight timeline. However, hearing others' reasoning on why they support or disavow a decision should be part of your ongoing duty to understand the impact of the decisions you've made.

  • Refactoring features that others just finished to meet personal expectations of standards, which demoralizes the dev who just completed the ticket

  • Doing more work by volume without communicating with the team about expectations, which prevents people from learning through trial and failure on tasks of differing difficulty --and also sets up a cycle of them expecting you will pick up their slack.

    • I've gathered my job is to show other devs how to do something better... and also give them the agency to decide if they want to follow. If they experience failure, this is something they need to realize and own as part of their career.

  • Simply delegating tasks without onboarding. Individual team members require context to complete their tasks. If you aren't able to find another person to sync with them, then you are responsible for equipping them to have the resources they need to complete their task.

  • Doing only the work that you're interested in, instead of thinking how you can do to support teammates and develop each person's technical and personal strengths.

    • This means allowing others to tackle challenging technical issues, not doing them because you are interested or have a high investment in them. You have other things to do cross-team, cross-department. You can step in to help when they can't figure it out.

Here are a few I've reflected on as being particularly unproductive for myself and the general productivity of a team:

  • Writing detailed tickets and docs and expecting folks to just read and follow suit, instead of helping them understand why writing initiative is important on a team, and to develop that muscle of writing down instructions to make others' lives easier when they have to review or test their work.

  • Expecting people to reply or make decisions right away, or to work at the same pace and style as you. You are the "fall guy/person" on the deliverables, but it's also your job to anticipate other folks' comfort level and work pace, how they feel motivated and empowered.

I've mentioned weaknesses I'm working to improve in this post. There's every chance feedback from one occasion, doesn't apply to a different situation, and one can overcorrect too.

I believe that reflecting on missteps creates opportunities for people to provide an alternate point of view for whether your own assessment on your performance is accurate, or overly critical.

I love working with people, and it's quite a shift how I've gone from working as an introvert scrappy solo artist to feeling like I need a group of people to work with to create anything.

All these are luxuries and privileges to be able to meditate on how I get to do what I love, and better, as work.

Questions for developers, quality analysts and PMs who are leading with or without authority and tech leaders out there:

  • What have you noticed yourself doing to cope with stress, and how has that impacted your team?

  • What have you tried to do to make others feel safe and understand how to work better together?

  • How have you handled less communicative or introverted personalities who don't share the same communication style?

  • What are moments for you where you realized a team is not functioning as you thought, and what have you done or changed in your approach to help them?

Sebastian Tiedtke - Sep 29

ProgrammingBuddyClub - Sep 30

MindsDB, brings machine learning capabilities to traditional databases. It acts as an AI layer on top of the existing tables and enables to train models and predict outcomes easily and instantly with the help of simple SQL statements.

MindsDB connects with the most popular DB Clients, BI Tools, Data Sources making it really easy for clients to use. You can use MindsDB locally (Docker, pip) or use MindsDB Cloud which provides a 30-day free trial.

item.title: 为什么在你的项目中使用 Golang。 | Why Use Golang For Your Project.
item.description:
尼拉吉·库马尔

发表于这是对 Go 的理想之处的更高层次的概述：

  • 云和网络服务
  • 命令行界面

二进制可执行文件

  • 能够编译为单个可执行二进制文件意味着：

  • 不需要运行时解释器，因此二进制文件可以比项目子目录的嵌套更苗条。 这有利于容器化和编排性能。
    执行不需要额外的运行时，因为它是机器代码，因此二进制可执行文件可以执行和恢复弹性。

极简主义者

  • 如果您玩过围棋的基础知识，您会注意到该语言并没有试图过于复杂或令人印象深刻。 完成工作就足够了。

自动垃圾收集

  • 因此，您可以专注于更重要的方面，而不会在性能上做出太多妥协。 不是每个人都喜欢自动垃圾收集的想法，但生产力是这里的主题。

格式

  • 有一个内置的格式化引擎，不需要使用“prettier.js”之类的东西，也不需要重新发明轮子。

内置测试和基准测试

  • 单元测试或基准测试不需要库，因此在进行各种项目协作时，您可能会有熟悉的测试和基准测试设置。

高级并发技术

  • 与 node.js 等异步平台中的并发相比，Go 的工作流程有了很大改进。

低样板代码

  • 联网

    • 这是属于 Go 项目的外部子存储库的一部分。

    最快的后端编程语言之一

    • 近年来，有几个基准测试（和咆哮）暗示 Go 后端应用程序有时比 Java 和 Rust 后端应用程序更快。 无需详述细节，我们就知道，Go 很快。

    尽管对软件开发中速度的重要性存在一些误解，但速度对于用户体验和开发成本至关重要。

    并发原语的并发系统实现简单一致的语言

  • 它是面向对象的
  • 带有编译器功能，有助于编译大型 Go 程序。
  • 有反向指针来解决编程中的各种问题
  • 标准库包
  • 构建具有简单结构和快速执行的 Web 应用程序
  • 支持无缝代码测试
  • 云计算支持和系统编程

使用 Golang 的缺点是什么？ 耗时的

它不是描述性的，非常简单。 因此，对于类似的功能，开发人员需要几行代码。 长时间的编码会话有时会使它变得单调。

这是一门年轻的语言
它正在庆祝它的 10 周年纪念日，它仍然是一门年轻的语言，而且这里的大量图书馆非常少。

由于它不支持通用函数，因此开发人员需要创建许多不必要的函数足迹。 而且，它可以帮助您了解 golang 在其他各个方面的用途。

原文:

Neeraj Kumar

Posted on Oct 22

This is a higher-level overview of what makes Go desirable for:

  • Web Development
  • Cloud & Network Services
  • DevOps & Site Reliability
  • Command-line Interfaces

Binary executable

  • IMO this is one of the most unappreciated aspects of Go. Being able to compile to a single executable binary means:

  • No runtime interpreter is needed so a binary can be far slimmer than a nest of project’s sub-directories. This is beneficial for containerisation and orchestration performance.
    No additional runtime is needed for execution as it’s machine code, therefore a binary executable can perform and recover with resilience.

Minimalist

  • If you’ve played with the basics of Go, you will notice that the language is not trying to be overly complex or impressive. It’s just enough to get the job done.

Automatic garbage collection

  • Go is a higher-level language with automatic memory management without the need for intervention. So you can focus on the more important aspects without too much compromise on performance. Not everybody likes the idea of automatic garbage collection, but productivity is the theme here.

Format

  • There is one built-in formatting engine, no need to use things like “prettier.js” and there’s no need to re-invent the wheel.

Built-in testing and benchmarking

  • Libraries aren’t required for unit-testing or benchmarking so you will likely have familiar testing and benchmarking setups when collaborating on a variety of projects.

Advanced concurrency techniques

  • Goroutines are like cheap (performance-wise) virtual threads that can be multiplexed across real threads. Goroutines, Channels, Mutexes, WatiGroups etc encourage patterns that allow parts of your codebase to talk to each other efficiently. Go has a vastly improved workflow when compared to concurrency in an asynchronous platform such as node.js.

Low boilerplate code

  • Go requires very little boilerplate code to create substantial applications, unlike Rust which requires far more boilerplate code when matching equivalent capabilities.

Networking

  • Go features a dedicated Networking API aimed at network programming built into it’s standard library. This is part of the external sub-repositories that belong to the Go project.

One of the fastest back-end programming languages

  • In recent years there have been several benchmarks (and rants) alluding to Go back-end applications sometimes being faster than Java and Rust back-end applications. Without dwelling on specifics, we get the picture, Go is fast.

Despite some misconceptions on the importance of speed in software development, fast is crucial for user experience and development costs.

The Features of Go Language are:

  • Concurrent system implementation of concurrency primitives Easy and consistent language
  • It is object oriented
  • Comes with compiler features and helps in compiling the big size Go programs.
  • Have back pointers to solve all kinds of issue in programming
  • Standard library package
  • Build web application with simple constructs and fast execution
  • Support testing for seamless code
  • Cloud computing support and system programming

What Are The Disadvantages Of Using Golang? Time Consuming

Its not descriptive and very simple. Hence, for similar functions the developers need lines of codes. A long coding session sometimes makes it monotonous.

It’s A Young Language
It is celebrating its 10 years of anniversary, still a young language and there are very less of extensive libraries here.

Golang Doesn’t Support Generic Functions
As it does not support generic functions hence the developers need to create many function footprints unnecessary. And, it helps you know what is golang used for in other respective.
